---
title: "Teaching"
lang: en
toc: false
---

<p class="justify">

Since 2022, I have taught undergraduate (Years 1–3) and graduate courses (Master’s level) to diverse audiences (economics, engineering, and environmental studies students), in lectures, tutorials, and computer-based classes (over 330 teaching hours in total). My teaching covers both <strong>core economics courses</strong> and <strong>more specialized topics</strong>, with particular attention to socio-&shy;ecological issues and transitions. I combine theoretical foundations, empirical analysis, and critical discussion of public policies, while promoting a pluralist and reflexive approach to economics. 

</p>

---

# Graduate Courses

## Environmental and Ecological Economics 

* <strong>Environmental Justice</strong> — Master’s level (Year 1), University of Paris Cité <br/>
<em>Primary instructor</em>

* <strong>Integrated Coastal Zone Management</strong> — Master’s level (Year 2), University of Bordeaux <br/>
<em>Teaching assistant</em>

# Undergraduate Courses

## Macroeconomics and Economic Theory

* <strong>Macroeconomics</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 1), University of Lille <br/>
<em>Teaching assistant</em>

* <strong>Macroeconomic Equilibrium and Policy</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 2), University of Paris Cité <br/>
<em>Teaching assistant</em>

* <strong>Economic Growth: Facts and Theories</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 3), University of Paris Cité <br/>
<em>Teaching assistant</em>

## Public Economics and Regulation  

* <strong>Competition Policy and Regulation</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 1), University of Lille <br/>
<em>Primary instructor</em>

* <strong>Public and Welfare Economics</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 2), University of Paris Cité <br/>
<em>Teaching assistant</em>

## Quantitative Methods and Data Analysis  

* <strong>Descriptive Statistics and Computing</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 1), University of Paris Cité <br/>
<em>Teaching assistant</em>

* <strong>Statistics for the Social Sciences</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 3), University of Lille <br/>
<em>Primary instructor</em>

* <strong>Advanced Quantitative Methods</strong> — Bachelor’s level (Year 3), University of Lille <br/>
<em>Primary instructor</em>
